name: Soy sauce (food medium)
basis: per 100 g (approx. per 100 mL; soy sauce density ~1.1-1.2 g/mL). Reference matrix = naturally brewed shoyu (soy + wheat).

components.name: water
components.source: USDA FDC 174277 (Soy sauce made from soy and wheat, shoyu; SR-Legacy 16124)
components.evidence: USDA moisture value; soy sauce is a dilute aqueous brine.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Dominant residual free reducing sugar in brewed shoyu; most koji-liberated glucose is consumed by yeast/LAB during moromi, leaving a small pool. Sweetened Chinese/amakuchi types run far higher (total sugars up to ~8 g/100g, incl. added sugar and melibiose; Zhang/Frontiers 2020 usukuchi total sugar 83.7 g/L).

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Minor residual disaccharide.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Not detected / negligible in plain shoyu.

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence: Not quantified; negligible in plain shoyu (melibiose-derived galactose can appear in some brews).

components.name: L-lactic acid
components.source: Frontiers Microbiol 2020 doi:10.3389/fmicb.2020.579808 (usukuchi 6008.52 mg/L); Devanthi & Gkatzionis review; Shodex KC-811 app note
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid, produced by Tetragenococcus halophilus LAB in moromi. Reported range 0.83-13.19 mg/mL across commercial samples; dark soy > light soy. Midpoint ~0.6 g/100g used.

components.name: acetic acid
components.source: J Food Sci 2018 doi:10.1080/10942912.2018.1497061 (Cantonese/Japanese/Sichuan mean 1.31-1.85 g/L)
components.evidence: Second major volatile acid; yeast/acetic-acid bacteria origin.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: Umami-associated diacid from yeast TCA overflow; consistently present.

components.name: pyroglutamic acid (5-oxoproline)
components.evidence: Formed by cyclization of free glutamate/glutamine during fermentation and heating; can rival lactic acid. Wide range 0-1.3 g/100g; midpoint used.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: Present but low, often below detection in brewed shoyu; included with null amount.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Shodex KC-811 soy sauce organic-acid app note
components.evidence: Detected at trace level by HPLC; not reliably quantified.

components.name: formic acid
components.source: Shodex KC-811 soy sauce organic-acid app note
components.evidence: Minor acid detected by HPLC; trace.

components.name: pyruvic acid
components.source: Shodex KC-811 soy sauce organic-acid app note
components.evidence: Glycolytic intermediate detected at trace level.

components.name: ethanol
components.source: Kikkoman technical statements; Fukushima, Soy Sauce review; ScienceDirect 'Soy Sauce' overview
components.evidence: Defining metabolite of naturally brewed koikuchi shoyu: yeast (Zygosaccharomyces rouxii) ferments wheat-derived sugars to ~1.5-2.5% (w/v) ethanol; acts as preservative and aroma precursor. Alcohol-free/reduced products are the exception.

components.name: L-glutamic acid
components.evidence: Highest amino acid; the umami driver. Free (unbound) glutamate specifically ~0.4-1.3 g/100mL (Umami Information Center: traditional shoyu 782-1264 mg/100mL free glutamate). Value here is total (free + peptide-liberatable).

components.name: L-aspartic acid
components.evidence: Second most abundant amino acid.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Largely hydrolysed to free amino acids and small peptides by Aspergillus oryzae koji proteases; amino-acid sum ~7.1 g/100g is highly bioavailable N. (Some USDA mirror revisions list ~8.1 g.)

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Genuine osmoprotectant present in soy sauce; compatible-solute relevance under the high-salt matrix. Not reliably quantified.

components.name: sodium
components.evidence: DEFINING feature of the medium: ~15-18% NaCl brine; imposes very high osmotic/ionic stress (~2.4 M Na+). Halotolerance is a hard growth constraint on this food.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Counter-ion to the ~15-18% NaCl; molar Cl- approximates Na+ (~2.4 M).

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Total Fe (mix of Fe2+/Fe3+ in matrix).

components.name: molybdate (molybdenum)
components.evidence: Genuine ultratrace micronutrient; not quantified for soy sauce. Retained at null.

components.name: ammonium
components.evidence: Free ammonia/ammonium from deamination during fermentation; contributes to total/formol nitrogen. Not separately quantified in USDA.

components.name: niacin (B3)
components.evidence: Most abundant B vitamin in shoyu.

components.name: vitamin B12
components.evidence: Absent (plant/koji fermentation without B12-producers).

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid but overall fat is minor (~0.6 g/100g); from residual wheat/soy lipid.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-nutritive ultratrace/contaminant element, not a defined growth nutrient of soy sauce; FooDB noise.

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: Furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae (parsley/celery/parsnip); biologically implausible in a soy+wheat ferment. FooDB predicted-not-measured noise.

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Non-physiological stereoisomer artifact / duplicate of L-isoleucine; FooDB predicted noise, not a distinct nutrient.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Free cobalt ion listed at ultratrace with no quantitation and no B12 in the matrix; FooDB noise rather than a real growth nutrient (retain only if a validated Co value appears).

missing_added: L-lactic acid (dominant organic acid, was absent)
citric acid / malic acid / formic acid / pyruvic acid (trace, null)
quantitative amounts for glucose and sucrose
quantitative amounts for all free/total amino acids (glutamate dominant)
quantitative amounts for all minerals (esp. ~15-18% NaCl / Na+ ~2.4 M)
quantitative amounts for all B vitamins
keep_uncovered: isoflavones (genuine soy isoflavones, no BiGG met)
glycine betaine (osmolyte; kept as a component with null amount)
epsilon-polylysine (genuine antimicrobial peptide additive in some brands)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 174277 (Soy sauce made from soy and wheat, shoyu; SR-Legacy 16124) - full nutrient panel incl. sugars, amino acids, fatty acids, minerals, vitamins
Frontiers in Microbiology 2020, doi:10.3389/fmicb.2020.579808 (organic acids, sugars, ethanol in Japanese soy sauces)
Journal of Food Science 2018, doi:10.1080/10942912.2018.1497061 (organic acid quantitation: lactic, acetic, succinic, pyroglutamic)
Shodex KC-811 application note - HPLC organic-acid profile of soy sauce
Umami Information Center - free glutamate content of shoyu (782-1264 mg/100mL)
Kikkoman / Fukushima soy-sauce reviews - ethanol content of brewed koikuchi shoyu (~1.5-2.5% w/v)
notes: Soy sauce is a high-salt (~15-18% NaCl; Na+ ~2.4 M) hydrolysed-protein brine. Dominant growth substrates are FREE AMINO ACIDS/peptides (glutamate first, then aspartate) as combined C+N, plus lactic acid (dominant organic acid) and residual glucose as carbon; ethanol (~2%) is a defining fermentation metabolite. The critical growth constraint is osmotic/ionic stress from the salt, so only halotolerant organisms grow. Sugars are low in plain shoyu but much higher in sweetened Chinese/amakuchi types (added sugar + melibiose).
